regime_justification: USDJPY recovering to 156 after MOF intervention last Thursday; spread still wide at ~187 bps but volatility elevated; oil easing sharply on US-Iran deal progress but BOJ remains behind the curve with only 2 intervention windows left under IMF rules.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: MOF intervened May 1 at 160.50; pair now consolidating near 156 after $35B yen-buy; IMF-constrained to 2 more 3-day operations by November; risk reversals and pace still flagging intervention threat zone.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: Intervention hangover caps USDJPY but BOJ on hold until H2 2026; oil cratering -13% removes immediate pressure

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Pair likely to retest 158-160 as carry differential remains wide; MOF has limited ammunition (2 episodes left)

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: June 15-16 BOJ meeting: interim JGB taper review but no hike expected; spread compression stalled

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If US-Iran deal confirmed and oil stays sub-$95, imported inflation pressure eases; carry unwind risk moderates near-term

bottom_line: Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention) persists: MOF spent ~$35B last Thursday to knock USDJPY from 160.50 to 156; pair consolidating but carry spread remains 187 bps and BOJ not hiking until H2. Oil plunge to $90 (US-Iran deal progress) removes acute import-inflation pressure, but authorities face IMF constraint—only 2 more 3-day intervention windows by November. Grades hold C/C/C/D: watchful through May-June as intervention deterrent fades and pair likely re-tests 158-160; unwind risk moderates if oil stays low and deal confirmed. Crypto flat—BTC $81.5k awaiting macro catalyst.

trigger_map.note: Conducted May 1 (~$35B). Only 2 more 3-day windows allowed by IMF rules through Nov. Likely re-arms if USDJPY approaches 160 again.
Next meeting Jun 15-16 (interim JGB taper review). Market prices no hike until H2 2026; but 3/9 board members dissented for hike Apr 28.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: Already at 2.50%, highest since 1997. Further break toward 2.7% (next resistance) would signal unwind acceleration.
Currently 76.8, up 10% today but well below 100 regime-change threshold.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: Oil cratered -13% today to $90 on US-Iran deal optimism. Shock risk now receding unless deal collapses.
5-day change now -0.93% (post-intervention stabilization). MOF historically reacts to pace not level; watch for re-acceleration.

narrative.p1: USDJPY consolidated near 156.40 today (-0.93%), digesting last Thursday's MOF intervention that pushed the pair from 160.50 to sub-156. Oil collapsed -12% to $90 as US-Iran ceasefire holds and Washington paused Project Freedom, signaling deal progress. US 10Y fell 5 bps to 4.38%; JGB 10Y flat at 2.50% (1997 high). VIX dropped -3.7% to 16.7 (complacency). No stage change—still Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): authorities showed willingness to act at 160 but face IMF constraint (only 2 more 3-day intervention windows by November per MOF official Monday). BOJ April 28 meeting kept rates at 0.75% (6-3 vote, 3 dissented for hike), raised core CPI forecast to 2.8%, cut growth to 0.5%. Next BOJ meeting June 15-16 (JGB taper interim review), no hike expected until H2 2026.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 188 bps (down 5 today but still wide—carry intact). Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (USDJPY 1M IV data stale). ¥/bbl computed = ¥14,043 (down 13%—import inflation pressure easing sharply). USDJPY 5-day pace -0.93% (post-intervention consolidation; was +1.7% pre-intervention). Distance from 60d mean: neutral (consolidating in 155-158 range). We remain Stage 2: intervention demonstrated MOF's 160 line, but ammunition limited (IMF rules) and carry differential still incentivizes shorts. Grades C/C/C/D: applied unwind prior at 1w and 2w (would otherwise grade D given oil collapse), because spread remains wide, BOJ behind curve, and intervention effect likely fades within 2 weeks as pair re-tests 158-160. Grade 3w C (Jun BOJ meeting risk, though no hike priced). Grade 4w D (if US-Iran deal confirmed and oil stays sub-$95, acute pressure moderates). Key divergence: oil relief is genuine, but policy divergence (Fed hold vs. BOJ slow normalization) keeps structural carry pressure in place.
narrative.p3: BTC $81.6k (+0.6%), XRP unavailable. Crypto sideways—whale addresses holding (per search), no flush yet. BTC correlation with DXY and risk-on muted; sitting below late-2025 highs (~$126k) as macro uncertainty (yen carry, Iran war) kept flows cautious. Oil plunge today is risk-on bullish in isolation, but crypto tracking equities more than FX lately. If yen stabilizes 155-158 and S&P continues melt-up (hit fresh highs Monday), BTC likely grinds higher toward $85-90k. Unwind scenario (Stage 4) would see BTC flush to $70-75k as liquidity drains. For now, holding pattern—no catalyst to break either way. Positioning: watch BTC open interest (direction unavailable today) and 30d correlation with DXY for early signal of risk-off rotation.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Intervention deterrent fades as market tests MOF resolve. Pair likely drifts back toward 158-160 as carry spread (188 bps) and BOJ inaction incentivize shorts. Oil relief (now $90) removes acute pressure, but policy divergence persists. MOF constrained (2 windows left). Grade C both weeks—watchful, not imminent unwind but setup fragile.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: June 15-16 BOJ meeting: interim JGB taper review. No hike expected but risk of hawkish language if inflation stays 2.8%+. If USDJPY re-tests 160 before meeting, MOF may burn another intervention window. Oil trajectory critical—if stays sub-$95, grade holds C; if spikes back above $110 on deal collapse, upgrade to B. Grade 3w C (scenario-weighted: 60% consolidation, 30% slow grind higher, 10% intervention #2).
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: If US-Iran deal confirmed (Washington optimism per search) and oil settles $85-95 range, imported inflation pressure dissipates and yen carry unwind risk moderates near-term. BOJ could delay next hike to Sep-Oct. Spread compression stalls but doesn't reverse. Grade 4w D (unlikely unwind within 4 weeks absent exogenous shock). Caveat: if deal collapses and oil spikes back above $115, all bets off—flip to B.

watch_list: USDJPY retest of 158-160: pace of rebound will determine if MOF intervenes again (only 2 windows left)
US-Iran deal confirmation: if announced this week, oil likely stays sub-$95 and yen pressure eases structurally
BOJ June 15-16 meeting rhetoric: watch for hawkish shift if 3+ board members continue to dissent for hike
MOVE index: currently 76.8, watch for break above 100 (rates vol regime change signal)
JGB 10Y break above 2.60%: would signal unwind acceleration as carry positions reprice BOJ trajectory
